creator: Alexander Terp
description:
The `in` operator returns false when checking if a list exists within a list of lists, even though equality comparison works correctly.

**Repro:**
```
items = [[1, 2], [3, 4]]
print([1, 2] in items)    // false (WRONG - should be true)
print([1, 2] == items[0]) // true  (equality works fine)
```

**Root cause:** In `core/expr_ops.go` lines 302-305, the OpIn case for RadList vs RadList calls `coercedLeft.Contains(right())` instead of `coercedRight.Contains(left())`. The operands are swapped compared to every other `in` implementation.

**Test files:** `bugs/operators/110_list_in_list.rad`, `110b_list_in_check.rad`, `bugs/collections/57_list_in_comparison.rad`, `71_list_in_list.rad`, `123_list_in_list_deep.rad`
title: in operator broken for list-in-list checks (operands swapped)
